feed.title: Adithya Vijay
feed.subtitle: Full stack software developer at QBurst.

entry.title: Stock Market Crash of 2008
entry.summary: Introduction In this blog, I’ll try to explain the events that led to the 2008-2009 financial crisis or the 2008 Stock Market Crash. The Great Recession of 2008 The Great recession refers to the global economic trumoil that occurred between 2007 and 2009. This led to a loss of almost trillions of dollars and affected the lives of many people directly and indirectly. It led to a lot of people l...

entry.title: Microsoft Azure Fundamentals
entry.summary: Introduction This blog contains the concepts of Microsoft Azure Fundamentals required to take the AZ-900 certification exam. Cloud Concepts (20-25%) [MODULE - I] Cloud computing is the delivery of computing services over the internet, enabling faster innovation, flexible resources, and economies of scale. Resources can be provisioned and released very easily. Cloud Models There are differ...

entry.title: Data Structures and Algorithms in C#

link.title: Data Structures and Algorithms in C#

entry.summary: Introduction Abstract Data Type An abstract data type is a class. Data structures are classes that are storing data and acting on it. Classes contain representation of data and operation on data. Big-O Notation This determines how efficient a piece of code is. It is a way to describe the speed or complexity of a given algorithm. O(1) - “Constant” time complexity. It means the code is the m...

entry.title: Basic Authentication
entry.summary: Introduction In this blog, we’ll discuss about how we’ll store passwords in a database, the basics of authentication and jwt tokens. Authentication is verifying that the user is who he says he is. How to Store passwords in a database Password can be saved in different ways : Storing in clear text - Never save password in clear text Hashing the password - Apply a hashing algorithm to the p...

entry.title: Building the skeleton of an Angular 12 and .NET 6 Application - Part 2

link.title: Building the skeleton of an Angular 12 and .NET 6 Application - Part 2

entry.summary: Introduction In this blog, we’ll be looking at how to build the structure of an application with .NET 6 as the backend and Angular 12 as the frontend. This is the second part of the blog where we’ll be creating the Angular 12 application. Please read part one of the blog here on how to create the .NET 6 API. Versions Angular has a major release every 6 months. The current lastest version when ...
